etymology.description: 廣(廣) (guǎng, "broad") + 東(東) (dōng, "east") + 話(話) (huà, "speech; language") forms the name for a specific regional dialect often used by the people in the Guangdong province, leading to its designation.
etymology.interpretation: 廣(廣) (guǎng, "broad") and 東(東) (dōng, "east") together refer to the Guangdong province, a region in the southeastern part of China known for its expanse to the east. 話(話) (huà, "speech; language") indicates the spoken language. Thus, 廣東話 (Guǎngdōnghuà) signifies the language of the broad eastern region, i.e., Cantonese, the specific language used predominantly by people in Guangdong.

examples.chinese: 很多香港人会说广东话(很多香港人會說廣東話)。
examples.pinyin: Hěnduō Xiānggǎng rén huì shuō Guǎngdōnghuà.
examples.english: Many people in Hong Kong can speak Cantonese.

examples.chinese: 我学习广东话(我學習廣東話)，因为我想去广东旅行(因為我想去廣東旅行)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xuéxí Guǎngdōnghuà, yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng qù Guǎngdōng lǚxíng.
examples.english: I study Cantonese because I want to travel to Guangdong.

examples.chinese: 听懂广东话(聽懂廣東話)对我来说有点难(對我來說有點難)。
examples.pinyin: Tīng dǒng Guǎngdōnghuà duì wǒ lái shuō yǒudiǎn nán.
examples.english: Understanding Cantonese is a bit difficult for me.
